target.name: Verkor
target.founder: Benoit Lemaignan, Christophe Mille, Sylvain Paineau, Philippe Chain, Gilles Moreau, Olivier Dufour

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $2.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

private.name: Amogy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $700M

data.narrative:
Verkor is a Series C climate & energy company that has raised over €3B to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe comprising one public comparable (Linamar, ticker LNZA) and eight private peers operating in the same sector and stage, including KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy.

The peer-median method is applicable and forms the primary triangulation approach. Three same-sector, same-stage comparables—KoBold Metals ($2.0B), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B), and Amogy ($700M)—yield a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range spanning $700M to $2.0B. This method directly addresses Verkor's positioning within the Climate & Energy Series C cohort.

Given the limited methodological triangulation (one applicable approach), the analysis yields a range of $850M to $1.1B, centered on a $1.0B median. The peer-median estimate of $1.0B carries primary weight. Key uncertainties include the heterogeneity of technologies and capital intensity profiles within the Climate & Energy sector, the materiality of Verkor's capital raised relative to peers, and the limited applicability of the single public comparable. A more robust analysis would benefit from additional valuation methods (revenue multiples, DCF analysis, or cost-of-capital benchmarking) to narrow the range and reduce reliance on peer medians alone.
